question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on (i) the current spread of avian influenza in Northern Ireland; and (ii) the actions his Department has taken to seek to reduce cases of avian influenza.
answer_text: The last case of avian influenza in poultry in Northern Ireland was in February 2022.  The last case of avian influenza in captive birds in Northern Ireland was in October 2022.  The last case of avian influenza in wild birds in Northern Ireland was in September 2023.Northern Ireland, the Republic of Ireland and Great Britain are all recognised as free from Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPA).Officials are continuing to work closely with their counterparts in the Republic of Ireland and across Great Britain to monitor the situation, engaging with stakeholders as appropriate to ensure the key messages of biosecurity and vigilance are promoted. The DAERA website https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/ai will be updated with the latest information as appropriate.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the potential benefits that the Belfast Region City Deal will bring to the regeneration of Bangor.
answer_text: My officials are working collaboratively with colleagues in the Ards and North Down Borough Council to support the development and delivery of the £72.8m Bangor Waterfront Regeneration Programme funded by the NI Executive through the Belfast Region City Deal.The Council’s proposals will regenerate 2.2 miles of Bangor’s waterfront from Skippingstone Beach to Ballyholme. The Outline Business Case for the programme, submitted by the Council, details the economic impact and aims to achieve an estimated additional 489 jobs, increase visitor numbers by 100,000 each year and increase GVA by £110 million, as well as providing environmental and health and wellbeing benefits. My Officials are also working with Council colleagues on the development of a Social Value Action Plan for the project, which will further enhance the aspirational economic benefits.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will commit to encouraging Translink to increase late night travel options with the opening of the new Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:“Translink are committed to increasing night time travel options from Belfast Grand Central and more widely within the network. We are currently planning a "Nightmover" service offering in the run up to Christmas 2024.  An ongoing increase in night time services, however, would require adequate funding to act as a pump primer to allow passenger numbers to grow and make the service self sustaining.”Due to the current budgetary position, the Department is unable to provide additional funding to Translink to support late-night services. You will be aware that my Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government, and currently there is no scope to increase services of this scale without securing additional funding. I can assure you however that the value of our public transport services cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the discussions he has held, or intends to hold, with officials from UEFA in relation to ensuring that Northern Ireland is involved in the delivery of the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship.
answer_text: I remain committed to securing a role for Northern Ireland in the EURO 2028 Tournament and I will be engaging in due course with partners to pursue this objective.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any meetings he has held with the Belfast City Centre Night Czar in relation to the support he can offer to sustain the nightlife in the city.
answer_text: Departmental officials met with Michael Stewart, Belfast’s Night Czar, on 26 June to discuss the night-time economy and what can be done to support its development. Subsequently, my officials recently attended a meeting of the Purple Flag Committee. Purple Flag is an international accreditation programme that aims to reward those who help to create safe and thriving locations at night for all users. The committee is attended by stakeholders in the night-time economy, including the Night Czar. Engaging with stakeholders, such as the Night Czar, will be ongoing and pivotal as we seek to promote not only Belfast’s nightlife, but that of all our towns and cities.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the draft Programme for Government 2024-2027 will be considered by the Assembly following public consultation.
answer_text: Yes, the consultation on the draft Programme for Government ended on the 4th November.  Work is now ongoing to analyse all responses received and produce a consultation report for consideration by the Executive. Once this has been considered, we will finalise the Programme for Government and bring it to the Assembly for cross-community approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has considered introducing a ban on the ownership of XL Bully dogs, following the ban recently announced in the Republic of Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his assessment of the regular occurrence of a burst water mains on the same section of the B511 Bangor Road, Groomsport; and (ii) whether there are any plans to replace vulnerable water mains pipes at this location.
answer_text: The management of the public sewerage network is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:(i) NI Water is aware that there has been an issue with bursts at this location on occasion. However, NI Water has stated that the bursts are not frequent enough to allow for replacement.(ii) NI Water uses a process of risk assessment and performance history to prioritise investment in all distribution watermains across the North.  Based on that process, each watermain is assigned a prioritising score, which includes burst frequency, water quality, and customer complaints. This results in the most critical watermains obtaining investment first.  NI Water regularly reviews the scoring in relation to mains replacement. If the performance of the water main changes, then it will be identified in future assessments.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com.  Making direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of why main grade prison officers from the pre-2002 intake are not in receipt of the same risk allowance as other officers.
answer_text: The Prison Service Pay Review Body published a report which introduced the Supplementary Risk Allowance. At paragraph 2.10, it stated “all the parties confirmed that Prison Service staff on pre-2002 pay and conditions had the NIPSP (Northern Ireland Prison Service Payment), generally considered to compensate staff for the difficult and dangerous environment in Northern Ireland, already incorporated into their basic pay”. As explained in my response to AQW 11581/22-27, as a result of the 2023 pay award, the total remuneration for CPO and MGO post 2002 at band maximum exceeded that of MGO pre 2002. All MGO pre 2002 staff were provided with the individual choice of moving to the CPO / MGO post 2002 pay scale and receiving arrears backdated to 1 April 2023. Those who decided not to transfer at that time, may still do so, at a time of their choosing from any current or future date.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 13435/22-27, AQW 12571/22-27 and AQW 8258/22-27, and given the number of serious and fatal injuries caused by XL Bully dogs in residential settings, whether he will consider further legislation to provide protection in residential settings where XL Bully dogs are kept.
answer_text: I am aware of the member’s concerns about potential attacks by XL Bully dogs in residential settings.  My Officials have considered the potential for further legislation in this area and determined that the only safeguard that could be deployed would be to require XL Bully dogs to be muzzled while at home. However, I believe that requiring a dog to be muzzled in a household setting would not be feasible, practical or enforceable and would severely impinge upon an owner’s ability to look after and enjoy time with their dog in a private dwelling. The legislation introducing safeguarding measures for XL Bully type dogs will protect public safety as it applies to when the dog is in a public setting and is aimed at preventing serious attacks in a public place.  However, if Assembly colleagues or stakeholders advocating for further legislation to provide protection in residential settings where an XL Bully dog is kept can provide viable suggestions, my officials will consider any such detail received.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 12693/22-27, for more detailed clarity on why the resurfacing scheme on the Groomsport Road roundabout went over the scheduled timeframe by over eight weeks.
answer_text: In the original press release for this scheme, it was announced that preparatory work would commence on 19 February 2024 and be completed by 29th March 2024, and that resurfacing works would be planned for after the Easter holidays. No dates for the resurfacing were specified within the press release. The scheme was completed by 31st May 2024 - a few days after the original programme completion date of 26th May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to confirm that his announcement, and the public signage, in relation to the Groomsport Road roundabout works stated that the project in totality would last six weeks.
answer_text: In the original press release for this scheme it was announced that preparatory works would commence Monday 19th February and be completed Friday 29th March 2024, and that resurfacing works would commence after the Easter holidays. Whilst the preparatory works were identified as taking a total of 6 weeks, the scheme also involved resurfacing works which were identified in the original press release as programmed for after Easter to mitigate against any increases in traffic volumes during the holiday period. The scheme was never reported as being a 6 week programme in its totality.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a breakdown of the cost to carry out one full grass cut of roadside verges in North Down.
answer_text: Grass cutting in the Ards and North Down area, like most Council areas, is undertaken by a combination of the Department’s internal contractor and external Environmental contractor.  The Department does not collate financial information relating to specific works undertaken by our internal contractor and therefore the cost of a single grass cut in a particular area is not available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 10972/22-27, to detail (i) whether the works at the Groomsport Road roundabout had any extra expense due to the scheme going over its predicted timeframe; and (ii) the final cost of the works.
answer_text: The majority of works undertaken by my Department are valued using schedules of rates with set rates for each item of work such as kerbing, road marking, square meters of resurfacing etc. The final measurement for the works at Groomsport Road has not yet been agreed with the contractor, however, it is expected that the cost of the scheme will be under budget. The duration of the scheme has no bearing on that cost.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in light of news of recent attacks by XL Bully dogs in household settings, whether he has given consideration to legislation that will provide protection to residents in a home where an XL Bully dog is housed.
answer_text: Pursuant to my replies to AQWs 12571 and 8258 on the matter of XL Bully type dogs in household settings, it remains the case that owners are responsible for controlling their dog, both at home and in public.  The legislation providing safeguarding measures for XL Bully type dogs, including the requirements for the animal to be muzzled and on a lead, apply to when the animal is in a public setting.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on discussions with the Crown Estates in relation to outstanding land issues with the Queen's Parade project in Bangor.
answer_text: My officials, in the Department’s Regional Development Office, along with Ards and North Down Borough Council, are continuing to work closely with the developer, Bangor Marine, and the Crown Estate in an effort to progress the development scheme. There continues to be positive engagement between the Crown Estate, Ards and North Down Borough Council and the Developer, Bangor Marine in relation to the proposals for Marine Gardens.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has had any discussion with the producers of the Blue Lights television programme in relation to how the proposed sale of Netherleigh House may interfere with its use as a filming location going forward.
answer_text: My officials in NI Screen are in regular contact with the producers of Blue Lights regarding all aspects of the production, including suitable filming locations, and will continue to provide assistance to the producers as they plan for future series.The Netherleigh House site was advertised on the open market in February 2024, a preferred bidder has been selected and sale is expected to complete over the summer. It would be for the production company to discuss meanwhile use with the new owners of the site following completion of the sale.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline what assurances he can give that all pupils with special educational needs will receive a placement in their school of choice in the 2024-25 school year.
To ask the Minister of Justice to outline how she intends to monitor terrorism-related offenders throughout this mandate.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister what engagement they had with Executive Colleagues in relation to the budget, to identify a process that better assesses need and risk.
answer_text: The Minister of Finance takes the lead on these matters.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why the Groomsport Road roundabout resurfacing scheme in Bangor, which was scheduled to last six weeks, is still ongoing after 14 weeks.
answer_text: The scheme commenced on 19 February 2024 and, with the exception of the final surfacing, was substantially complete on 19 April 2024. Messaging to the public provided a 6 week window for preparatory work with further closures required after the Easter period to undertake the resurfacing element of the scheme.Given the high traffic volumes and nature of this work the resurfacing of the roundabout was scheduled over weekends to minimise disruption to the travelling public. The final resurfacing was completed on 27 May 2024 with only a few minor items of work such as topsoiling of verges and setting of ironwork completed by 31 May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail what discussions his Department has with public transport providers in advance of major road closures.
answer_text: My Department carefully plans all closures on the road network, along with the proposed diversionary routes, to minimise the traffic disruption caused. Translink, along with other relevant agencies, are notified of each closure as they are approved, well in advance of any works taking place.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in light of fatal attacks by XL Bully dogs in residential settings, whether he plans to provide protection for the families of owners of these dogs within the home.
answer_text: The forthcoming legislation for XL Bully type dogs will require owners to appropriately record and account for their dog, to have third-party insurance regarding that dog, to meet certain conditions in public, and to keep their dog in a secure place when not in public.The Dogs (Northern Ireland) Order 1983 currently enables councils to impose control conditions on dogs where an officer has reasonable cause to believe that an offence has been committed.  For example, one such condition would require that the dog is kept securely confined in a building, yard or other enclosure. It remains the case that owners are responsible for controlling their dog, both at home and in public.  My Department has published advice and guidance on ‘responsible dog ownership’ and ‘sharing your house with a dog’ and it is intended that this information will also be included in upcoming messaging on XL Bully type dogs.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department collects data from schools in relation to bullying.

To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for an update on the redevelopment of Queens Parade, Bangor; (ii) what preparation work has been undertaken to date; (iii) when the development will begin; (iv) to detail the forecast date of completion; and (v) what impact the rising cost of materials will have on the project.
answer_text: My officials, in the Department’s Regional Development Office, along with the Ards and North Down Borough Council, are continuing to work closely with the developer, Bangor Marine, and all other stakeholders in an effort to progress this scheme. The Bangor Marine team is working through the pre-commencement conditions that emerged from the planning approval, and there has been positive engagement with the Crown Estate Commission regarding the proposals for the Marine Gardens area of the project. To prepare for construction commencing later this year, Bangor Marine removed trees and shrubbery in and around the current Marine Gardens Car Park in advance of the nesting season. Once the contractors are on-site, the most recent plan from Bangor Marine shows that construction is expected to take in the region of 4 years. The rising cost of materials is an issue across all building projects, and the Bangor Marine team continues to closely monitor costs to ensure that delivery of the project is in line with current plans and approvals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of reports that £5.4 million of outstanding fines have been transferred to collection services, what plans are in place to ensure that court fines are paid.
answer_text: The Fine Collection and Enforcement Service (FCS), which is part of the Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS), was established on 1 June 2018 and is responsible for the enforcement and recovery of unpaid fines. Where a court makes a collection order, FCS collection officers use a range of powers as set out in the Justice Act (2016) to collect and enforce outstanding financial penalties. These powers include granting instalment orders or additional time to pay, applying for deductions from benefits, making attachment of earnings orders, freezing money in a debtor’s bank account and recommending to the court a bank account order or vehicle seizure order.As of 31 March 2024, FCS have recovered £14.5m in outstanding financial penalties of which £4m was recovered in 2023/2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will consider creating more car parking spaces to meet the needs of elderly residents in Ardgheean Gardens, Kilcooley, Bangor.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The issue of a lack of availability of parking spaces within our estates is a common one. We review such requests in terms of demand, the number of Housing Executive properties remaining in the locality and the availability of funding and land to accommodate additional spaces. The matter had been raised with the local office and a negative decision has been made in the provision of additional spaces.” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what actions he has taken to ensure the impact of water shortages, due to drought, on the farming community and the food production industry are minimised.
answer_text: Annually, CAFRE delivers Knowledge Transfer and Innovation programmes to over 13,000 farmers and farm family members. This has included training, support and advice to farmers and growers on dealing with the impact of climate extremes and preparing their businesses to mitigate the impact.During previous periods of drought, CAFRE has provided support and advice through the delivery of training events and the production of technical articles to help farmers minimise the impact of water shortages upon food production. In terms of preparedness for exceptional weather events, my officials continue to engage and collaborate with government and agency partners such as the Northern Ireland Flood Strategy Steering Group and the Regional Community Resilience Group to ensure that our collective response to, and recovery from adverse weather, whether flooding or drought, is efficient, effective and targeted. To complement this engagement within NI Civil Contingency structures, my Department also maintains an Agri-Food Supply Chain Contingency Plan that can be activated in the event of a significant incident, including water shortage. This Plan can be triggered if the circumstances are of sufficient severity to result in considerable interruption to local agri-food supply chains.  As the impact of climate change becomes more evident, it has become increasingly important to encourage farm business owners to better manage risk within their own enterprises and in doing so become more resilient and better prepared to cope in situations of extreme weather, such as drought. It is for this reason that a Crisis Framework is being developed as a key element of my Department’s new Farm Support and Development Programme. The Framework will enable my Department to assess potential industry impacts such as adverse weather or market failure and identify the most appropriate timing and form of intervention necessary for a specific crisis.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what the impact will be on waiting times following the decision of the Executive to allocate the £34 million already committed by the United Kingdom Government prior to the restoration of the Executive compared to his targeted bid for waiting list initiative spend in 2024-25 of £215 million.
answer_text: The £34m which has been earmarked for waiting lists – which is coming directly from the UK Government and which had already long been announced - does not even cover half of what is required to maintain red flag (cancer) and time critical patients. My officials are currently working with Trusts to examine if it is possible to displace other hospital services to the fund the amount of the remaining investment needed (£41-46m). That in itself will cause significant damage and disruption. If we are unable to fully do so, it will lead to longer waiting times and reduce the amount of care available to red-flag and time critical patients and that would be a deplorable situation to find ourselves in.This is also a missed opportunity to improve waiting lists where significant progress could have been made. There has been no additional funding at all provided for tackling our waiting lists. Had I been allocated the funding I had bid for, there would have been an initial focus on those people waiting more than three years. Over 28,000 patients waiting more than 3 years for treatment would have been treated and over 57,000 patients waiting more than 3 years for a first outpatient appointment would have had an appointment.Instead, our waiting lists will continue to grow, which stands in stark contrast to other supposed commitments that tackling them was going to be a priority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline when local football clubs can expect to receive a grant from the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.

To ask the Minister for Communities, in relation to Northern Ireland being named as a joint host of the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship, whether his Department has developed an alternative plan in the event of Casement Park not being rebuilt on time.
answer_text: Contingency plans in relation to host stadia for the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship are a matter for UEFA.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the salary difference paid to prison officers in the pre-2002 intake and the new recruits who joined the prison service after this date.
answer_text: In previous evidence to the Prison Service Pay Review Body, I highlighted the need to address pay differentials. I am pleased with the progress that has been made. Senior Officers pre-2002 and post-2002 grades are on one common pay scale. Custody Prison Officers (CPO) and Main Grade Officers (MGO) post 2002 grades moved to one common pay scale in 2021. Their total remuneration was then aligned to Main Grade Officer (MGO) pre 2002 in 2022. In 2023, the total remuneration for CPO and MGO post 2002 at band maximum exceeded that of MGO pre 2002. All MGO pre 2002 staff were provided with the individual choice of moving to the CPO / MGO post 2002 pay scale and receiving arrears for 2023. Those who chose not to transfer may still choose to do so from a current date.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, in relation to Northern Ireland being named as a joint host of the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship, whether their Department has developed an alternative plan in the event of Casement Park not being rebuilt on time.
answer_text: The redevelopment of Casement Park remains an executive flagship project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in relation to Northern Ireland being named as a joint host of the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship, whether his Department has developed an alternative plan in the event of Casement Park not being rebuilt on time.
answer_text: My Department, working with the Department for Communities (DfC), the Irish Football Association (IFA), the Ulster Council of the Gaelic Athletic Association (UCGAA) and other key stakeholders, remain committed to delivering the European Football Championships in Belfast during the summer of 2028.This includes the hosting of a designated number of tournament games at Casement Park. However, in the event that any of the proposed host stadiums are not deemed suitable, it will be for UEFA to develop the necessary contingency plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the timeline for his Department taking action to resurface Castle Park Road, Bangor.
answer_text: The programme for 2024-25 will be developed and published once future budgets have been agreed. In the meantime, officials in my Department will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the impact of the temporary closure of Great Victoria Street train station on railway commuters travelling to and from North Down.
answer_text: As I outlined in my statement to the Assembly on 16th April 2024, my Department and Translink have been working closely to seek to minimise the impact of the opening of Belfast Grand Central Station. The disruptions, including the closure of Great Victoria Street train station, to the wider public, business community and surrounding communities, have taken account of all public transport users, including those in North Down. Whilst the development of these mitigation measures has been informed by an extensive and detailed assessment using the best available models and data, the overall effectiveness of the measures will depend on the public changing their current travel patterns by choosing alternative modes and times for their journey or by taking a different route to avoid congested areas in the vicinity of the construction works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether train passenger capacity will be increased to take account of the reduced railway timetable introduced by Translink during the rebuilding of Great Victoria Street train station.
answer_text: As I outlined in my written statement to the Assembly last month, a revised rail timetable, which includes increased train capacity, was introduced following the closure of Great Victoria Street Train station on 10th May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of whether attempts to obtain additional budget funding from HM Treasury will be successful in this current fiscal year.
answer_text: The Interim Fiscal Framework, which I signed along with the Chief Secretary to the Treasury on 21 May, marks significant progress on the current position within the financial package that accompanied the restoration of the Executive. It recognises that that the Executive will continue to plan on the assumption that it will be funded at or above the 124% level of relative need in future financial years. This avoids the financial cliff-edge once the current financial package ends in 2025-26. The Interim Fiscal Framework also applies the 124% needs-based adjustment factor to new Barnett consequentials arising from the date of the restoration of the Executive.As a result, there is now an additional £24 million from Spring Budget that will be available for allocation as part of June Monitoring, and any additional Barnett consequentials that the Executive receives in 2024-25 will be uplifted by this needs-based factor.I will continue to make the case for increased funding for public services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the plans his Department has in place to manage the disruption to traffic and associated road closures during the pending resurfacing of the Groomsport Road roundabout, Bangor.
answer_text: The Groomsport Road roundabout scheme commenced on 19 February 2024 and has progressed significantly and is now ready for the final surface course.  A full road closure has been planned to resurface the roundabout over two consecutive weekends; 18th and 19th May, and, 25th and 26th May 2024, weather permitting.  Careful planning and consideration has been given to this to help minimise traffic disruption.  Details of the closure will be communicated to the public on Variable Message Signs leading up to the location of the works and on my Department’s Trafficwatchni website. This will include information of the alternative routes. In addition, the contractor will provide marshals during the works to assist the travelling public.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department plans to bring forward legislation in relation to hunting with dogs for consideration in the Assembly.
answer_text: I am personally supportive of the need to ban the hunting of wild animals with dogs.However, I need to consider the priority of any relevant legislation against other priorities within my Department.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of whether attempts to obtain additional budget funding from HM Treasury will be successful in this current fiscal year.
answer_text: Discussions are ongoing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the current hotel capacity to meet future demands of tourism for bed nights.
answer_text: Current hotel accommodation capacity is 143 hotels, with 9,637 bedrooms, offering a combined total of 21,173 bedspaces. Occupancy has come back to pre-pandemic levels, reaching 74.4% in 2023.In terms of new developments, there are currently nine projects that are on site.  Approved projects may get underway in 2024 but are unlikely to start trading before 2025. There are likely to be around 200 rooms coming into the market in 2024, a rise of 2% and the same level as 2023.Forecasting the future beyond 2024 and giving any meaningful figure on hotel numbers is difficult, particularly as the last number of years have been so unpredictable. The NI Hotels Federation anticipate that the sector will grow and advise that not all projects will come to fruition but there are opportunities and keen interest in investing. Growth will be driven by the Private Sector and will bring more jobs, help transform local communities and produce economic wealth.As part of my Tourism Vision and Action Plan I will support the development of more hotels outside of Belfast. Following the review of Invest NI, my Department is working with Invest NI and Tourism NI to transfer the responsibility for providing capital support for new tourist accommodation from Invest NI to Tourism NI. As part of this process, I will be reviewing my Department’s policy for this support to ensure that we can meet the future demands of tourism for high quality accommodation across the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the proposal for the York Street Interchange project.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) why resurfacing works have paused at the Groomsport Road roundabout, Bangor; and (ii) when work to resurface the road itself will recommence.
answer_text: The scheme commenced on 19 February 2024 and, with the exception of the final surface, was substantially complete on 19 April 2024. A full road closure is required to lay the final surface and, to minimise traffic disruption, this work has to be done over a weekend.The final surfacing has been programmed for Sunday 26 May 2024, weather permitting.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when resurfacing work at the Groomsport Road roundabout, Bangor, will be completed.
answer_text: The scheme is scheduled for completion on Sunday 26 May 2024, subject to favourable weather conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any extra expense will be incurred as a result of not meeting the expected timeline of works for the resurfacing scheme on the Groomsport Road roundabout, Bangor.
answer_text: The resurfacing scheme at the Groomsport Roundabout is currently running as programmed and will be complete by 26 May 2024, subject to favourable weather conditions.No additional costs have been incurred because of the scheme's timeframe.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline what discussions he has had with international TV and movie production companies regarding the use of film and audio studio facilities and location sites in Northern Ireland for future projects.
answer_text: Oral Question No 15 – AQO 367/22-27I had the opportunity to meet with US screen industry executives during a recent visit to Studio Ulster, a new cutting-edge virtual production facility that is due to open later this year.We have the facilities, people and locations that can make a success of any screen production, from small independent projects to major film and television productions. My officials from NI Screen are in regular contact with international screen production companies as we seek to build on this success and secure new business for the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department plans to support members of the farming community struggling to find an opportunity to sow crops due to current adverse ground conditions.
answer_text: I am acutely aware that this year’s very wet and late Spring is presenting challenges for farmers operating in almost every sector within the industry.I am conscious of the wider impact if wet weather continues in the time ahead and I have made representations to the UK Government about the need for central government intervention if the situation lasts for much longer.  Meanwhile, my officials in the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise (CAFRE) continue to monitor the situation very closely, maintaining contact with stakeholders and farmers across the range of sectors within the industry. CAFRE has released a number of advisory articles on the management of farming operations following this period of prolonged inclement weather and tailored advice is also being offered at group meetings and through one-to-one discussions with individual farmers.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any solutions are being considered to make the Portaferry Road on the Ards peninsula safer during periods of adverse weather.
answer_text: During adverse weather conditions, notices and warnings are issued via the media by both my Department and the PSNI, advising the public to use an alternative route. Regrettably, even when the decision has been taken to close the road, a number of people chose to disregard this despite the significant risk it may pose to them.I would urge all road users to drive according to the weather / road conditions and heed advice from forecast providers, the Department, and the PSNI.  Motorists should never ignore road closures that are put in place for their safety.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the Chief Constable has had any discussion with her in relation to the proposal to close the Public Enquiry Office at Bangor PSNI Station.
answer_text: While I have regular engagement with the Chief Constable, the proposal to close the Public Enquiry Office at Bangor PSNI Station has not been discussed.The prioritisation of policing resources, including the closure of Enquiry Offices, is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational independence of both organisations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the call by the sole survivor and families of those murdered in the Kingsmill massacre for the establishment of a public inquiry.
answer_text: The Coroner, the Honourable Judge Sherrard, delivered Kingsmill findings on 12 April 2024. I wish to express my deepest sympathy to the sole survivor Alan Black, and to the families of those murdered in the 1976 attack.  I can only imagine the continuing pain and trauma of listening to the detail of the Coroner’s findings. Mr Black has called for a public inquiry, particularly highlighting his frustration around the impact of the Public Interest Immunity (PII) process on the inquest, as well as the role of the Irish Government. Both of these matters, that is, national security and international relations, are reserved matters and consequently the call for a public inquiry should be considered by the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland and the UK Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why his commitment, announced in February 2024, to publish road closures required in relation to the Groomsport roundabout resurfacing scheme in Bangor, has not been complied with.
answer_text: My Department is only required to advertise road closures in the local press when the duration of the closure is more than 2 weeks.The Groomsport Road Roundabout scheme was delivered predominantly using lane closures with 4 short duration road closures required to safely deliver the more challenging elements of the works. These closures were all less than 2 weeks and the time periods were also restricted to minimise disruption to the traveling public.In addition, the road closures were published on the Department’s own TrafficwatchNI website prior to work commencing. Variable Message Signs and various information signs were also erected to keep road users informed of the works.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline how many prosecutions have been taken as a result of alleged pollution incidents in the Cotton River, in the last ten years.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any Northern Ireland tourism initiatives were discussed during the ministerial visit to the United States of America in March 2024.
answer_text: I addressed a roundtable business breakfast in New York, organised by Tourism Ireland on 12 March 2024, attended by 20 key travel trade partners (including tour operators and airline contacts). Programming and selling the North as a must-see destination as part of a wider island of Ireland vacation package was the key topic of discussion, with trade partners agreeing that there is significant potential to grow their tourism business here. Discussions also involved upcoming signature events which might attract US visitors, including The Open taking place at Royal Portrush in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 8893/22-27, whether his Department will give any consideration to improving the current drainage capacity at the Ballysallagh Road bridge, Bangor.
answer_text: Previous instances of flooding at Ballysallagh Road Bridge, Bangor have been attributed to a damaged section of pipe that was remote from the road. Repair work was completed and my Department will keep the area under review. The are currently no plans to undertake any further works at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when his Department plans to remove the trees that have been cut down in the general area of the Cairn Wood car park.
answer_text: Forest Service anticipates that the trees felled in the area of Cairn Wood carpark will be removed from the site, as part of a more general recovery of trees along the Craigantlet road, later this year in the early autumn period. Forest Service is taking forward the necessary arrangements to undertake these operations, which involve securing the necessary road closure permissions and the appointment of a qualified contractor.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the signage erected recently in car parking areas attached to Crawfordsburn Country Park.
answer_text: The signage recently erected in car parking areas of Crawfordsburn Country Park relate to the recent introduction of a car park management scheme which is being managed by a private company.All signage that has been erected, is in compliance with the requirements of the British Parking Association (BPA) and the wording of signage has been subject to approval by BPA prior to use.The management scheme and associated signage is necessary to ensure the safe and appropriate use of the car parking facilities at the site, for example, by deterring parking that blocks site access to emergency vehicles (which has been a problem encountered at the park during busy periods).
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what checks are carried out in relation to the removal of road closure signage when work on major road projects are halted during breaks in operation of the work.
answer_text: The provision, maintenance and checking of signage at roadworks is the responsibility of the Contractor tasked with the scheme and under whose control the road remains during the works.Signage at roadworks where a road is closed during specific times is still required outside the hours of the closure if other works are ongoing or if there is a temporary road surface. Signs relating solely to the road closure should be covered or set aside when the road closure is not in effect.Where works are undertaken on behalf of my Department, the Contractor will be notified where signage does not meet the required standard, should this be identified during supervisory checks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why possible road closures are alluded to in connection with major road projects, but no advance notice is published when actual road closures are implemented.
answer_text: The requirement for the formal publication of a road closure is dependant on the length of the closure. Where a road closure has a duration of over 2 weeks, my Department will normally ensure that the closure is advertised in the local press.In addition, advanced notification signage or Variable Messaging Signs can be put out in advance of the works taking place. Details of road closures which are on A-Class roads would also normally be included on the Department’s TrafficwatchNI website.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the work that has been carried out to protect children from being exploited by paramilitary and criminal gangs.
answer_text: We refer the Member to the answer given to AQW 9497/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how medical care is delivered to those detained in secure prisons.
answer_text: Responsibility for healthcare in prison transferred from the Northern Ireland Prison Service to the Department of Health on 1 April 2008.  Healthcare services are commissioned by the Strategic Planning and Performance Group in conjunction with the Public Health Agency and delivered by the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has raised any concerns in relation to the newly-installed equipment in the proposed MOT Test Centre at Hydebank, Belfast.
answer_text: The equipment contractor, Worldwide Environmental Products Inc, completed the installation and commissioning of new vehicle testing equipment and associated software at the Driver & Vehicle Agency’s new test centre at Hydebank in early February 2024.  The new equipment and software is currently undergoing user acceptance testing to ensure it has been installed and commissioned in accordance with the contract specification.  This equipment and software will only be signed off when I have received assurances from officials that it operates safely to the required standard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what percentage of those currently detained in each secure prison are being held on remand.
answer_text: The information requested is shown in the table below for the prison population on 24 March 2024.Total PopulationRemand Population% RemandMaghaberry1,25559447.3%Magilligan49551.0%Hydebank Wood1346750%

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether elected representatives, speaking in either closed or public session of council meetings, have any level of protection against defamation claims.
answer_text: The law of defamation, while offering a range of defences to a defamation claim, does not afford absolute privilege for elected representatives speaking in Council meetings. Qualified privilege may be available under statute and common law, provided it can be shown that a statement was not made with malice. However, this is an area in which specific legal advice is advisable.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the funding available for waiting list initiatives in 2024-2025.
answer_text: Reducing waiting times for elective care is a priority for my Department, the Health and Social Care Sector and indeed the Northern Ireland Executive.  Over the last years, additional funding, under the auspice of Waiting List Initiatives, has been provided to the HSC. This funding allowed additional patients to be seen outside core funded services. As pressures in services have increased, Waiting List Initiative funding has been used not only to fix waiting lists, but also to plug the capacity / demand gap in red-flag (cancer) and time critical services.The budget for 2024/25 has not been confirmed, however, I have made the case to the Executive that minimum funding of approximately £75-80m per year will be needed to ensure those with cancer or time critical conditions can be treated as required. In addition, multiyear funding is needed to tackle the backlog of patients waiting – up to £135m per year.Recurrent sustainable financial investment in core capacity is also required to allow transformation of elective care services to help reduce demand, providing a more cost-effective way to reduce waits and to prevent the build-up of waits in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a breakdown of personal injury claims that his Department has paid out to claimants who injured themselves on pavements on Donaghadee Road, between Groomsport and Donaghadee, in the last five years.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department’s records indicate there have been no personal injury claims received for accidents on the Donaghadee Road in the last five years.  However, the Donaghadee Road becomes Warren Road approximately halfway between Groomsport and Donaghadee.  During this five-year period, my Department has received three claims for accidents on footways on Warren Road, one of which was for accident on an unadopted private footpath.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has an inspection strategy for (i) maintaining; and (ii) replacing damaged road traffic directional bollards.
answer_text: My Department carries out regular inspections of all public roads and footways to ensure that essential response maintenance is identified and completed as necessary. During these inspections all defects are noted, including for example defective or damaged traffic bollards.The frequency of these inspections and the subsequent response time depends on the type of road and the volume of vehicular or pedestrian traffic. Defective, damaged, or missing bollards will be categorised as urgent or non-urgent work with the priority given to safety related defects.The importance of a well-maintained road network cannot be understated but this requires investment. I will continue to seek the support of Executive colleagues to enable this to happen.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department's plans for (i) weed control on footpaths; and (2) roadside grass cutting, during the incoming growing season.
answer_text: My Department is committed to protecting wildlife and promoting biodiversity when managing roadside verges and our approach aims to maintain the right balance between road safety and the control of grass and weeds, while achieving a greater focus on environmental protection. With that in mind, in October 2022, I announced a new approach to verge management across the road network to protect the environment.From the 2023/24 year, a single swathe is being cut along the verges on the strategic road network twice per year. Other areas that are needed for road safety purposes, such as for sight lines at junctions, are also being cut at least twice each year. A similar approach has been introduced on heavier trafficked rural roads; and on lighter trafficked rural roads, where possible, the cutting frequency is being reduced to once per year and towards the end of the growing season.The new approach increases the Department’s focus on protecting wildlife and promoting biodiversity when managing roadside verges and will support the actions needed to comply with the Climate Change Act passed by the Assembly in 2022.The level of grass cutting and weed spraying that can be carried out in future financial years will depend on the 2024-25 final budget determination for my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any correspondence he has had with the United Kingdom government in relation to east west trade.
answer_text: I have attended the inaugural meeting of the East West Council on 26 March 2024, at which I discussed East West trade with the British Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any correspondence he has had with the Irish government in relation to cross-border trade.
answer_text: I have discussed north-south trade with Simon Coveney and met with IDA and Enterprise Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of discussions he had with potential investors during his St Patrick’s Day visit to Washington DC.
answer_text: In discussions with potential US investors, I was able to outline my economic vision and highlight the North’s unique tariff-free access to British and EU markets.I assured potential investors that they would benefit from the close cooperation between government, industry and academia. I was pleased to support Invest NI’s discussions around talent availability, skills development, the quality of our universities and successful track record of supporting international companies which have chosen to invest in the region. I am aware that investments from large corporations take time to secure in the face of substantial competition from other global locations, however I am confident that these discussions and the ongoing work of Invest will result in positive future outcomes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he is considering a lower age limit for those in charge of a XL Bully dog in a public place.
answer_text: I anticipate that the forthcoming legislation regarding XL Bully type dogs will require the animal to be muzzled and be on a lead, which is being held securely by a person no less than sixteen years of age, when in a public place.  I have no plans to lower this age requirement.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of discussions they had with potential investors during their St Patrick’s Day visit to Washington DC.
answer_text: The U.S. remains our largest and most important source of inward foreign direct investment.  260 U.S. owned businesses operate here employing over 30,000 people.We had a very positive experience, meeting business leaders from a wide range of sectors and delivered a clear message that we are an attractive investment opportunity for global companies.  Over the coming months we will continue to build on the connections we have made in the United States in order to grow our economy for the betterment of our citizens.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department will ensure that US businesses invest in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The United States is our largest source of Foreign Direct Investment. Over two decades and more that investment, measured in billions of dollars, has supported innovation and job creation across sectors such as Life and Health Sciences, Financial Services, Cyber Security and Software Development.Existing US investors speak freely about the pull factors that brought them here, not least our skilled workforce and world class research capabilities across several fields. They also recognise the role of Invest NI which, going forward, will be putting our unique dual market access proposition front and centre of its international sales pitch. There remains strong interest in the North among US investors – I, my officials and Invest NI will be working hard over the coming months to convert that interest into investments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how his Department can assist owners of electric vehicles in urban areas charge their vehicles at home, if they do not have a driveway.
answer_text: Home charging solutions for those people without access to off-street parking is one of the issues that has been raised by the EV Infrastructure Taskforce. Members of the group have highlighted potential engineering solutions and my officials are engaging with their counterparts to ensure they are fully informed in relation to learning from pilots taking place across other jurisdictions. However, this is not simply an engineering issue. As the Roads Authority responsible for street works, my officials must give careful consideration to wider issues such as the safety of those using our footways and issues around liability. In solving one problem we need to ensure that we are not creating another. While the Department considering how best to progress this particular issue, other solutions for those without access to a driveway are being actively progressed. Derry City and Strabane District Council is leading a consortium of councils on the provision of further on-street charging. The Consortium was successful in securing funding through the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) and match-funding support from my Department to install 127 charge points across nine Council areas which includes Newry, Mourne and Down District Council. In addition, our on street public charging infrastructure supports those without access to home charging and those travelling longer distances. The provision of this network is commercially led in the North. The most recently available statistics on this indicate that there are now nearly 550 publicly available charge points here which represents an 63% increase since August 2021. There have been 80 new chargers installed in the past two months, which is a rise of 17%.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) whether she will provide an exemption to residents of the structurally-defective apartments at Victoria Square, Belfast, from continuing to pay domestic rates; and (ii) whether she will refund rates paid during the period when residents have had to move out of their home.
answer_text: Land & Property Services will write to those impacted to inform them their properties will be removed from the Valuation List with no further bills issuing.  Ratepayers who had paid rates since vacating their property will be refunded.  For those that did not pay, rate arrears will not be pursued and any associated Court actions will cease.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, whether he will now issue a detailed response to AQW 5993/22-27.
answer_text: NI Water is responsible for the operational delivery of its day to day activities which are independently regulated by the Utility Regulator for Northern Ireland.  NI Water has a dedicated resource for queries from elected representatives.  Therefore, as the issue you previously raised was an operational matter for NI Water, the company is best placed to answer your question. You should therefore, direct your question to NI Water, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of whether the structurally-defective apartments at Victoria Square, Belfast, can be repaired.
answer_text: I have made no such assessment as this is a civil matter between private residents and the developer of the Victoria Square apartments.  As such, it would be inappropriate for me or the Department to comment on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has any plans to introduce legislation, similar to that in Great Britain, in relation to legal cases involving claims against structural defects in property, like those dealt with recently in the local courts involving apartments in central Belfast.
answer_text: Primary legislative responsibility on the time limits for bringing defective premises claims is not within my Department’s remit. Whilst it is currently the responsibility of the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, I understand that the Department for Communities has accepted that it should accept transfer of that responsibility, subject to Executive and Assembly approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will prioritise a new build for Bangor Central Integrated Primary School.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has plans to bring revenue raising schemes to the Executive for its consideration.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans he has to provide a permanent solution to the regular flooding under the bridge that spans Ballysallagh Road, Bangor.
answer_text: The recent flooding at Ballysallagh Road Bridge, Bangor has been attributed to a damaged section of pipe that is remote from the road. Repair work is now complete and my Department will keep the area under review.However, it should be noted that road drainage systems are only designed to cope with normal average annual daily rain fall and not the sudden high intensity rain events experienced in recent times and that there is no guarantee that flooding will not occur in the future despite my Department’s best efforts.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, whether he is now in a position to provide a detailed reply to AQW 8141/22-27.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“The information provided below by settlement as at 31st December 2023:Holywood60Donaghadee41Millisle22”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, whether he is now in a position to provide a detailed reply to AQW 7690/22-27.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“In Table1 below I have detailed the total new social housing completions in the North Down parliamentary constituency delivered via the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) for the period 2019/20 – 2023/24. In table 2 I have provided further details of these completions.Table 1: Completions 2019-2024YearTotal SHDP Completions2019/201032020/21762021/22142022/2302023/2420Total213The Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) is approved on a 3-Year rolling basis, so details of new social housing proposals for the North Down Parliamentary Constituency are only available for the period 2024/25 – 2026/27. Currently within the North Down Parliamentary constituency there are 61 social housing units under construction. Details of these are provided in Table 3.  There are 100 social housing units programmed to start as part of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) 2024/25 – 2026/27. The delivery of these and the wider Social Housing Development Programme will be subject to sufficient capital budget being made available to my Department. Details have been provided in Table 4. Please note that programmed schemes can be lost or slip to future programme years for a variety of reasons e.g. relating to delays in acquiring sites and/or failure to secure Planning Approval. Additional schemes can also be added to the SHDP ‘in-year’ through Competitive Land, Design & Build procurement route and/or the purchase of Existing Satisfactory / Off-the-Shelf properties.Table 2: Total new social housing Completions in the North Down Parliamentary Constituency 2019/20 – 2023/24Housing AssociationScheme NameLocationScheme TypeUnitsOnsite YearCompletionYearChoiceNorth Down Young People Leaving Care, Donaghadee RoadBangorNew Build122014/152019/20Choice14 Exchange MewsDonaghadeeExisting Satisfactory Purchase12018/192019/20Choice12 Exchange MewsDonaghadeeExisting Satisfactory Purchase12018/192019/20Choice6 Rathgill MeadowsBangorExisting Satisfactory Purchase12019/202019/20Choice35B Dufferin AvenueBangorExisting Satisfactory Purchase12019/202019/20Clanmil66a -78 Bloomfield RoadBangorNew Build482016/172019/20Clanmil66a -78 Bloomfield RoadBangorNew Build82016/172019/20HabintegThe Spencer, Church ViewHolywood Off The Shelf302018/192019/20RadiusLeaseholds, Phase 7BangorExisting Satisfactory Purchase12019/202019/20ClanmilCorner of Faulkner Road/Clandeboye RoadBangorNew Build522016/172020/21ClanmilCorner of Faulkner Road/Clandeboye RoadBangorNew Build62016/172020/21Clanmil375 Old Holywood RoadHolywood Competitive Land Design & Build142018/192020/21NB HousingMeetinghouse StreetDonaghadeeOff The Shelf42019/202020/21Ark18 Ballywalter RoadMillisleCompetitive Land Design & Build112019/202021/22NB Housing26 Ballyminetragh GardensBangorRehab12020/212021/22NB Housing70 Bal

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, whether he is now in a position to provide a detailed reply to AQW 7583/22-27.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Employee turnover rates for the Housing Executive remain significantly lower than reported UK average rates.  UK turnover rates as reported in September 2023* were 20.2% overall, and 18.5% for public services.  The Housing Executive monitors employee turnover rates both annually and across a 5-year period. The current 5-year average is 6.02% and the annual figure for 2023/24 was 6.4%. Whilst the Housing Executive figures have been trending higher over recent years, the figures remain relatively low. The Housing Executive’s workforce plan is reviewed twice a year and details planned resourcing activity both annually and over a 5-year period. During 23/24 it conducted 256 recruitment exercises, receiving 4205 applications, resulting in 250 appointments to date from these exercises.  726 appointments have been made during the last 3 financial years. The Housing Executive continue to develop and implement its workforce plan in partnership with each Directorate, delivering a significant resourcing programme to ensure that it meets business need in a challenging labour market. In addition, a number of other interventions designed to improve retention have been introduced including well-being initiatives, employee engagement action plans, flexible working arrangements and a review of the Housing Executive’s approach to reward and recognition.  (Note:  * Report published by Xpert HR (developed by Cendex - data analytics company). “For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has any plans to introduce a carer's recognition payment.
answer_text: I recognise the important role unpaid carers play within the wider community as well as the vital support they provide to those who they are caring for.I have noted the recommendations relating to additional support for carers that were made in the Independent Advisory Panel’s Report into the future of welfare mitigations that was published in October 2022, which include a recommendation in regard to a carer’s recognition payment. The recommendations made in that report and any future mitigation package will need to be assessed in the climate of financial constraint and the requirement to ensure that budgets remain sustainable over the coming years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has any plans to introduce a young carer's grant.
answer_text: I recognise the important role young carers play within the wider community as well as the vital support they provide to those who they are caring for.I have noted the recommendations relating to additional support for carers that were made in the Independent Advisory Panel’s Report into the future of welfare mitigations that was published in October 2022, including the introduction of a young carer’s grant. The recommendations made in that report and any future mitigation package will need to be assessed in the climate of financial constraint and the requirement to ensure that budgets remain sustainable over the coming years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has any plans to increase the earnings threshold for carers in work.
answer_text: The earnings threshold in Carer’s Allowance will increase from £139.00 to £151.00 per week in April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the anti-poverty strategy.
answer_text: Addressing poverty in all its forms is a key priority for me and I am committed to delivering long-term, sustainable solutions to poverty for our communities right across Northern Ireland.	I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to the Executive Anti-Poverty Strategy. Final decisions on the content and implementation of the Anti-Poverty Strategy will be subject to Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether football clubs who have already submitted a fund bid for the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer will be required to make a new bid.
answer_text: The Minister for Communities whether football clubs who have already submitted a fund bid for the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer will be required to make a new bid.   AnswerIn the 23/24 financial year the Department decided to make available a small pot of funding to assist some performance football clubs develop their proposals to modernise their facilities. The fund known as the ‘Football Ground Business Case Development Fund’ is a standalone scheme and is not part of the Sub-Regional Programme. Clubs who received funding to assist them develop business cases will have no guarantee that these business cases will be funded by Sub-Regional or any other funder. With regards to the Sub-Regional Programme, this programme is currently in the policy development stage and has not been open for a call for applications. I have asked my officials to present me with proposals and options for consideration to take this commitment forward as a matter of priority. Once final proposals are confirmed and approvals are secured, I will be able to provide details and a timeframe for the process to apply for funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his assessment of the structural condition of the road surface on the citybound stretch of the Sydenham bypass between the entrance to the George Best Belfast City Airport and the junction with Dee Street; and (ii) how many vehicles use this stretch of road (a) Monday to Friday; and (b) during weekends.
answer_text: Resurfacing schemes are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our roads in a reasonable condition. While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.The road surface on the citybound stretch of the Sydenham bypass between the entrance to the George Best Belfast City Airport and the junction with Dee Street, like many other roads, is going signs of deterioration and would benefit from a structural maintenance intervention.Whilst the resurfacing programme for 2024/25 is yet to be developed, it is my Department’s intention to include a scheme on Sydenham Bypass, subject to the availability of the necessary financial resource. In the meantime, officials in my Department will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s policy.The latest traffic data on this stretch of road is from 2020. It indicates that the average daily weekday flow was 33,596 vehicles and that the average daily weekend flow was 23,877 vehicles.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has concerns in relation to water quality in Belfast Lough.
answer_text: I am concerned at aspects of water quality in Belfast Lough, including the Shellfish Water Protected Area in the inner Lough and bathing water quality at locations such as Ballyholme and Brompton.  Bathing water quality is assessed under the Quality of Bathing Water Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008. Within Belfast Lough, the 2023 annual classification – based on faecal indicator bacteria - resulted in ‘excellent’ for Crawfordsburn and Groomsport, ‘good’ for Helen’s Bay, but ‘poor’ for Ballyholme and the candidate site of Brompton.  Water quality studies have shown that the main sources of faecal indicator bacteria are from both human and agricultural sources. Levels are affected by agricultural runoff and wastewater infrastructure.I will be considering proposals to improve water quality within the Third River Basin Management Plan, with a view to bringing it to the Executive. I will also consider the outworkings of the water quality review on Lough Neagh, and the reports of the Office for Environmental Protection and the Northern Ireland Audit Office, which are due to be published in the near future.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on measures being taken by his Department to address car parking issues on Warren Road, Donaghadee.
answer_text: I believe the Member is referring to an area on the Warren Road, Donaghadee that was previously the subject of AQW 6675/22-27, and I can confirm this matter is now with officials for them to carry out a safety assessment and consider if any measures are required to assist in the management of parking in the area

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) whether his Department's Marine Wildlife Team are unable to respond to out of hour calls reporting dead and stranded marine animals; (ii) to detail the team's operating hours; (iii) what arrangements are in place to deal with issues outside of operating hours; and (iv) what plans he has to improve this service.
answer_text: The Department’s Marine Wildlife Team does not respond to out of hours calls reporting for dead or stranded marine animals.The team’s operating hours are 9am to 5pm from Monday to Friday.Exploris Seal Sanctuary responds to the rescue of live stranded, injured and abandoned Harbour and Grey seal pups. The British Divers Marine Life Rescue (BDMLR), jointly with the Irish Whale and Dolphin Group (IWDG), responds to live stranded porpoises, dolphins and whales. Both organisations have a hotline number that can be used at any time. The removal of dead marine wildlife is at the discretion of the landowner or land manager.The Department provides financial assistance to these organisations, through the Environment Fund, to assist with the response to live stranded marine animals. I would urge any representatives with concerns about particular issues in terms of out of hours provision to highlight these to myself so that consideration can given in terms of any potential issues arising.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the process of moving livestock back and forth between Northern Ireland and Great Britain.
answer_text: Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2020/692 supplementing Regulation (EU) 2016/429 (Animal Health Law) and Regulation 2017/625 (Official Controls Regulation), which apply in Northern Ireland (NI) by virtue of their listing in Annex 2 of the Windsor Framework, set out the rules for the movement of livestock from Great Britain (GB) to NI. 		In October 2021, the EU Commission amended Regulation (EU) 2020/692 providing a derogation enabling NI cattle return directly from approved shows and sales in GB without needing to fulfil a residency period.		Livestock movement requirements from GB to NI are subject to: 	an Export Health Certificate (EHC) for all;	a supplementary certificate for breeding/production pigs/sheep/goats;	a specific import licence for breeding/production sheep/goats;all animals must be pre-notified to the NI port of entry at least 24 hours in advance via a CHED-A electronic notification on TRACES NT; and	all animals must be presented to the NI SPS Inspection facility for documentary, identity, physical and welfare checks.The UK Government provides financial support to UK traders moving agrifood goods and live animals from Great Britain to Northern Ireland.		The movement of livestock from NI to GB, i.e. cattle, sheep, goats, pigs and poultry are subject to: 	a commercial document and a general movement licence for poultry; and	an export health certificate for cattle, sheep, goats and pigs.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many (i) repair grants; and (ii) renovation grants have not been processed due to a lack of funding.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“(i) “There have been no repair grants cancelled due to budget restrictions.”(ii) “In the last two financial years the following number of grant enquiries, which were defined as renovation, were cancelled due to budget restrictions:Year2022/232023/24Renovation4185”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.I hope this information is useful.
